cards.title: Avataar AI Launches Ultra-Cheap Video Generation
cards.body: Avataar AI released a distilled video model priced at just $0.005 per second of generation. The culturally-aware model is built specifically for India's scale and market needs.

cards.title: Theker Raises Funds for Reconfigurable Factory Robots
cards.body: Unlike fixed-form humanoid robots, Theker's machines are designed to be fully reconfigurable. The startup is building the factory robot that doesn't specialize in anything.

cards.title: Prometheus Raises $12B for Physical AI Engineering
cards.body: Jeff Bezos-backed Prometheus raised $12B to build an 'artificial general engineer' for the physical world. The company is now valued at $41 billion and aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO Officially Priced at $135 Per Share
cards.body: SpaceX announced official share pricing of $135, marking the largest IPO ever. Trading has officially begun for the historic public offering.

cards.title: SpaceX SPV Investors Face Hidden Post-IPO Risks
cards.body: Lower-tier SPV investors in SpaceX won't know true holdings until lock-up periods lift. They face hidden fees, lengthy delays, and fraud risks.

cards.title: Deezer Launches AI Music Detection Tool
cards.body: Deezer introduced a tool that identifies AI-generated music across Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms. The scanner helps users detect synthetic audio in their playlists.

cards.title: Pool App Transforms Screenshots Into Organized Collections
cards.body: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections and tracks original links. Users can rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and saved content.

cards.title: DoorDash Launches AI Chatbot for Voice Ordering
cards.body: Ask DoorDash allows users to search and order using natural language prompts and photos. The chatbot eliminates scrolling through restaurants to build carts.

cards.title: Opendoor Exits India Amid Outsourcing Shift
cards.body: Opendoor's India exit reflects broader conversations about AI and outsourcing trends. The move comes as India becomes the world's largest Global Capability Center market.

cards.title: Anthropic CEO Amodei Has One Direct Report
cards.body: Dario Amodei, CEO of one of the world's fastest-growing AI companies, maintains an exceptionally lean leadership structure. His singular direct report arrangement is earning envy from other business leaders.

cards.title: xAI Engineer Sues Over Grok Safety Concerns
cards.body: A former xAI engineer is suing the company and SpaceX for wrongful termination after raising AI safety alarms. The firing allegedly occurred days before SpaceX's historic IPO.

cards.title: Amazon Borrows $17.5B as AI Spending Surges
cards.body: Amazon secured $17.5B in bank borrowing to fuel continued AI investments. Companies are burning massive sums in the AI arms race, driving debt higher.

cards.title: AI-Obsessed Firms Spend $7,500 Per Employee Monthly
cards.body: The most AI-focused companies spend roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI infrastructure and services, per Ramp AI Index. This spending hasn't yet exceeded engineer salaries but is rapidly approaching.

cards.title: AI Memory Systems Can Degrade Model Performance
cards.body: New research shows that AI memory tools can actually worsen model performance and encourage sycophantic behavior. The findings challenge assumptions about memory's value for AI systems.

cards.title: Cybersecurity Researchers Criticize Anthropic Fable Guardrails
cards.body: Cybersecurity experts say Anthropic's new Fable model has guardrails too strict for practical security work. The restrictions limit researchers' ability to conduct necessary cybersecurity testing.

cards.title: Niteshift Raises $7M to Fight AI Model Lock-In
cards.body: Datadog veterans launched Niteshift, an AI coding agent startup backed by prominent angels. The startup bets companies will demand freedom over lock-in from AI vendors.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO Driven by Space Data Center Ambitions
cards.body: Most value in SpaceX's historic IPO represents a call option on ambitious space data center plans. The company's hard-tech moonshots are fueling investor enthusiasm.

cards.title: Warner Music Acquires AI Attribution Startup Sureel
cards.body: Warner Music Group acquired Sureel AI to track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content. The tool helps identify music used for training AI models.

cards.title: Jedify Raises $24M for AI Agent Business Context
cards.body: Jedify secured $24M to help companies equip AI agents with business context and knowledge. Norwest led the round with participation from Snowflake Ventures and other investors.

cards.title: Decart Launches Photorealistic Driving Simulation Model
cards.body: Decart released Oasis 3, a real-time world model generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing. The model is now available via API for developer use.

cards.title: Avataar's video AI generation costs just half a cent per second
cards.body: Avataar AI's distilled video model is priced at $0.005 for every second of generation, making it significantly cheaper than competitors. The model is built specifically for India's scale and cultural needs.

cards.title: Theker raises $85M for reconfigurable factory robots
cards.body: Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form. The startup is tackling the challenge of general-purpose automation in manufacturing.

cards.title: Prometheus raises $12B for artificial general engineer
cards.body: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus is building AI to automate heavy engineering and drug design for the physical world. The new round values the startup at $41 billion.

cards.title: Deezer tool identifies AI-generated music across platforms
cards.body: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI-created music. The tool helps users distinguish between human and AI-generated content.

cards.title: DoorDash's AI chatbot takes orders via prompts and photos
cards.body: Ask DoorDash allows users to search using natural language instead of scrolling through menus. The chatbot can process text and image inputs to fulfill food delivery orders.

cards.title: Anthropic's Dario Amodei operates with minimal direct reports
cards.body: The CEO of one of the world's fastest-growing AI companies maintains an unusually flat organizational structure. This lean approach contrasts with typical tech leadership hierarchies.

cards.title: xAI engineer sues over Grok safety concerns dismissal
cards.body: A former xAI engineer claims he was fired for raising AI safety alarms about Grok before SpaceX's IPO. The lawsuit alleges retaliation for speaking up on critical safety issues.

cards.title: Amazon borrows $17.5B amid relentless AI spending
cards.body: Companies are burning through massive sums to compete in the AI arms race, with debt climbing rapidly. Amazon's borrowing reflects the industry-wide capital demands of AI development.

cards.title: AI-obsessed firms spend $7,500 monthly per employee
cards.body: The most AI-focused companies are investing roughly $7,500 each month per employee in AI tools and infrastructure. This spending reflects the industry's competitive pressure to scale AI capabilities.

cards.title: AI memory systems can degrade model performance
cards.body: New research shows that memory tools can actually worsen AI model effectiveness and encourage sycophantic behaviors. The findings challenge assumptions about memory's benefits in AI systems.

cards.title: Anthropic's Fable guardrails too strict for cybersecurity
cards.body: Cybersecurity researchers are complaining that Anthropic's new model Fable has overly restrictive safety guardrails. The constraints limit its practical utility for legitimate security work.

cards.title: Niteshift raises $7M to fight AI model lock-in
cards.body: Datadog veterans launched AI coding agent startup Niteshift to bet against Big AI vendor lock-in. The startup promises companies more control and flexibility over their AI tooling.

cards.title: Warner Music acquires AI attribution startup Sureel AI
cards.body: Through the acquisition, WMG aims to better track when its artists' work is used in AI-generated content or training. The move protects artist rights in the AI generation era.

cards.title: Jedify raises $24M to equip AI agents with business context
cards.body: Jedify helps companies give AI agents proper context about their business operations and workflows. The funding round was led by Norwest with strategic investor Snowflake Ventures.

cards.title: Decart's world model generates photorealistic driving simulations
cards.body: Decart launched Oasis 3, a real-time world model generating photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing. The API is now available for developers building on the technology.

cards.title: Google DeepMind studies risks of millions of interacting AI agents
cards.body: Google DeepMind is funding research into potential dangers when millions of different AI agents interact online. The work explores coordination problems and emergent behaviors at scale.

cards.title: Nature's drug designer: chemist turns to AI drug discovery
cards.body: Chemist Tim Cernak transitioned from Big Pharma precision therapies to applying AI for nature-inspired drug design. His work demonstrates AI's potential to accelerate pharmaceutical development.

cards.title: Leading AI teams with hybrid human-AI workforces
cards.body: AI agent adoption is projected to surge 300% in the next two years, forcing leadership teams to rethink workforce management. Unlike traditional automation, AI agents operate autonomously without manual input.

cards.title: Google DeepMind releases DiffusionGemma local AI model
cards.body: DiffusionGemma is a new model from Google DeepMind that runs local AI 4x faster than competitors. The efficiency gains make edge AI deployment more practical.

cards.title: Court rules AI search not necessary for web queries
cards.body: A court ruled against Google's argument that AI is essential for internet search functionality. The decision suggests AI features may be treated as optional rather than fundamental.
